encounter.chiefComplaint: New-onset anxiety symptoms affecting sleep and concentration before finals

clinicalNote.subjective: 18-year-old male presents with new-onset anxiety symptoms affecting sleep and concentration before finals. Reports increased worry, difficulty sleeping, and low mood. Episodic cannabis use disclosed. Denies SI/HI.
clinicalNote.objective: BP 118/74 mmHg, HR 88 bpm, RR 16, Temp 36.9°C, SpO2 99%. Weight 68.0 kg, Height 175 cm. PHQ-9: 11 (moderate depression). GAD-7: 13 (moderate anxiety). SI/HI negative on structured assessment.
clinicalNote.plan: Begin SSRI trial (sertraline titration as documented) with counseling on activation side effects and serotonin syndrome education. Refer to campus counseling / CBT. Reduce caffeine; minimize cannabis during SSRI initiation. PHQ-9/GAD-7 in 4 weeks; urgent follow-up sooner for worsening mood or SI. Discussed crisis resources.
clinicalNote.patientInstructions: Call crisis line or go to ED for suicidal thoughts, inability to care for self, or panic attacks with chest pain/syncope. Do not stop SSRI abruptly.

medications.name: sertraline
medications.dose: 25 mg daily x 7 days then 50 mg daily

restrictions: Limit refined sugars and ultra-processed foods
dietPlan.notes: Mood-supportive diet rich in omega-3s, magnesium, and complex carbohydrates. Avoids substances that worsen anxiety or depression.

agentContext.oneLineSummary: 18M with moderate depression/anxiety on new SSRI; substance use and caffeine counseling documented.
keyFactsForTools: SI/HI negative today — repeat safety assessment at follow-up.
Drug interaction check: sertraline + THC/alcohol sedation risk.
Track PHQ-9/GAD-7 trend for dose adjustments.
